creator: inyaoo@gmail.com
text:
When checking licenses the framework forgets to remove temporary files.

There are two types of temporary files: with `portslicense' and
`portlicenses' prefixes. The former is created every time by `!=' when
conditions are met and the latter only during ask-license target.

Reported by: naddy[1]

[1] http://docs.freebsd.org/cgi/mid.cgi?imcvcd$2ve8$1

Fix: As port makefiles are sourced several times I couldn't track why
_LICENSE_ASK_DATA keeps being created when ask-license is not invoked.
Probably, at that time only the line with `!=' is reached. To workaround
this just unlink() the file shortly afterward, it'd be created by `>>'
by sh(1) later, anyway.

I'm using `trap' shell builtin as dialog(1) can be interrupted by user
via SIGINT or sent SIGTERM by its parent, user or via shutdown procedure.
How-To-Repeat: 1. Run `make LICENSES_ASK='
2. select "view" then "accept"
3. interrupt the build
4. continue build with LICENSES_ASK= still defined
5. run `ls /tmp/port*'
